mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) is a coincident indicator of consumer demand; persistent strength translates directly into revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS month-over-month growth fails to produce positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate is above 9%, elevated slack suppresses wage-driven consumption and signals weak corporate pricing power, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 9%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 1% and consumer prices are rising month-over-month, the resulting negative real interest rate environment pushes institutional capital out of cash and short-term Treasuries into equities, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: zero lower bound with inflation uptick
failure_condition: Joint occurrences of FEDFUNDS < 1% and rising CPIAUCSL fail to produce positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction term is insignificant in a multivariate regression (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During post-crisis recovery, month-over-month increases in industrial production signal capacity utilization normalization and operating leverage recovery, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In months classified as post-crisis recovery, rising INDPRO coincides with negative 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of observations, or the information coefficient is negative over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 600 thousand units indicate a stalled construction sector and weak household formation, which drags on durable goods and employment expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST < 600, the proportion of negative 20-day forward returns falls below 45% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous month-over-month rise in nonfarm payrolls and fall in the unemployment rate indicates a genuine tightening of labor market conditions rather than a participation-rate artifact, causing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through stronger consumption forecasts.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increasing month-over-month and UNRATE decreasing month-over-month
failure_condition: Joint month-over-month increases in PAYEMS and decreases in UNRATE are followed by negative 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of instances, or the interaction term adds no marginal explanatory power (incremental R^2 < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 0.25%, sustained near-zero rates signal emergency monetary accommodation that compresses the equity risk premium, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS < 0.25% periods do not produce positive 5-day forward returns in at least 55% of instances, or Sharpe ratio of the signal is negative over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Three consecutive monthly declines in industrial production foreshadow inventory liquidation and margin compression across the manufacturing sector, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Three consecutive monthly declines in INDPRO are followed by positive 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the cumulative return conditional on the trigger is not significantly negative (bootstrap p > 0.05).
